question: Which 3GPP specification is the primary security standard for LTE/EPS?
How many distinct security feature groups (types) are defined in the LTE security architecture?
Which LTE security feature type covers Network Access Security?
How many distinct keys are used in the LTE key hierarchy?
What is the length of the master key K in the LTE key hierarchy?
What is the length of the K_ASME key in LTE?
What cryptographic function is used as the Key Derivation Function (KDF) in LTE?
Which of the following correctly describes the LTE key derivation chain? Choose all that apply.
Why are SIM cards NOT allowed in LTE networks?
options: SIM cards only support one-way authentication and use weaker keys
SIM cards are physically too large for LTE devices
SIM cards do not support IP-based communication
SIM cards cannot store a subscriber identity
SIM cards are not manufactured anymore
correct_answers: SIM cards only support one-way authentication and use weaker keys

question: What is the purpose of the GUTI in LTE security?
options: It is a Globally Unique Temporary Identifier used instead of the IMSI to protect user identity
It is the key used to derive NAS encryption keys
It identifies the P-GW assigned to the UE
It is the authentication token sent during AKA
It uniquely identifies the eNodeB in the network
correct_answers: It is a Globally Unique Temporary Identifier used instead of the IMSI to protect user identity

question: How many steps are in the LTE AKA (Authentication and Key Agreement) procedure?
Which of the following are threats to LTE network security? Choose all that apply.
options: Jamming attacks on the radio interface
Malware on UEs, RAN, or Core
correct_answers: Jamming attacks on the radio interface
Malware on UEs, RAN, or Core

question: How did the security trust model evolve from 2G to 4G LTE?
options: 2G mutual trust → 3G user distrust → 4G 'in doubt, all distrust'
2G no auth → 3G mutual trust → 4G user distrust
2G user distrust → 3G mutual trust → 4G full trust
2G one-way auth → 3G no auth → 4G mutual auth
Trust model has not changed across generations
correct_answers: 2G mutual trust → 3G user distrust → 4G 'in doubt, all distrust'
